title: Disocactus × mallisonii
intro: Disocactus × mallisonii, also known as Mallison's night-blooming cereus, is a hybrid cactus species belonging to the Cactaceae family, characterized by its funnel-shaped flowers and nocturnal blooming habits. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Disocactus × mallisonii include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbalism, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory problems and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Disocactus × mallisonii is a hybrid species of flowering plants in the cactus family (Cactaceae). It is a cross between Disocactus alseuosiformis and Strophocactus schulzianus. This hybrid species is primarily found in Mexico, where it grows in the wild. The classification of Disocactus × mallisonii is further supported by its unique physical characteristics, such as its short stems and funnel-shaped flowers. As a hybrid, its classification is also a subject of ongoing study and refinement in the field of botany.
